manual.description: SAS-signed HTTP trigger that receives the purchaser's AS2 MIME payload + AS2 headers. Trigger name is kept 'manual' (build-lock 5) so CI extracts this trigger's callback URL and injects it into the purchaser app setting SupplierAs2EndpointUrl (KV ref). Body JSON schema is left empty because AS2/X12 content arrives as flat file.

Decode_AS2.description: AS2 (v2) built-in Decode: decrypt + verify the purchaser's signature against the supplier AS2 receive agreement 'Supplier-Purchaser-AS2' (resolved by AS2-From/AS2-To in the message headers). Produces the base64 decoded 850 payload and a signed synchronous MDN to return in the HTTP Response. Native in-app action (type AS2Decode, no serviceProviderConfiguration, no connections.json entry) mirroring the verified AS2Encode shape.

Respond_With_MDN.description: Return the signed synchronous MDN (headers + content) from Decode_AS2 with HTTP 200 so the purchaser's outbound AS2 POST succeeds. Placed immediately after Decode_AS2 (per MS Learn B2B tutorial) so the transport ACK is returned even if downstream business processing fails. The 997 functional ACK is sent separately as a fresh AS2 message.

Decoded_850_FlatFile.description: The AS2 (v2) decoded payload is base64-encoded JSON string content. Convert it to the X12 850 flat-file string once, so it can be fed to X12 Decode and used to read the 850's group/transaction control numbers for the 997 echoes (base64ToString per MS Learn B2B 'Add the decode X12' Standard example).

Decode_X12_850.description: X12 built-in Decode of the 850 flat file (operationId x12Decode, JSON param messageToDecode). Resolves the supplier X12 receive agreement 'Supplier-Purchaser-X12-850' by ISA/GS identities from the linked Integration Account and validates against schema X12_00603_850. Native in-app action (type X12Decode, no serviceProviderConfiguration, no connections.json entry) mirroring the verified X12Encode shape. Good transaction sets appear in output goodMessages, invalid ones in badMessages.

Build_997_Xml.description: Explicitly build the 997 XML (root X12_00603_997) for a version-consistent 006030 acknowledgment (build-lock 1: do NOT rely on the X12 Decode auto-generated 4010 997). Happy-path structure ST-AK1-AK2-AK5-AK9-SE (accepted). Control-number echoes are read from the decoded 850 flat file per Simon's rule of record: AK102 <- 850 GS06, AK103 <- 850 GS08, AK202 <- 850 ST02; AK101='PO'/AK201='850' are the locked functional-group/transaction-set ids. ST02/SE02 are placeholders overwritten by the send agreement's control-number generator. Global elements (AK1/AK2Loop1/AK2/AK5/AK9) are X12-namespace-qualified; local ST/SE and data fields are unqualified, per Simon's X12_00603_997.xsd.

Encode_997_to_X12.description: X12 built-in Encode of the 997 XML at 006030 (operationId x12Encode). Resolves the supplier X12 send agreement 'Supplier-Purchaser-X12-997' (GS01=FA) by name from @appsetting('X12SendAgreementName'); the agreement owns/generates the 997's own ISA13/GS06/ST02 (distinct from the AK102/AK202 echoes). Native in-app action mirroring the verified X12Encode shape.

Encode_997_to_AS2.description: AS2 (v2) built-in Encode of the 997 interchange: sign + encrypt and request a synchronous MDN per the supplier AS2 send agreement. Direction is reversed from the 850: as2From=SUPPLIER01, as2To=PURCHASER01. Native in-app action mirroring the verified AS2Encode shape.

POST_997_to_purchaser.description: POST the signed+encrypted AS2 997 payload + AS2 headers to the purchaser's inbound-997 callback URL (KV-referenced app setting Purchaser997EndpointUrl). Reuses the verified AS2 output accessors messageHeaders / messageContent.$content from the working send workflow. The purchaser returns its own MDN in the response; success/failure is captured as a tracked property (own-action output only).

Record_997_Send_Failure.description: Failure handler (build-lock routing): an HTTP-triggered workflow has no Service Bus lock to settle, so a failed/timed-out 997 send is captured here for run-history visibility. Runs only when POST_997_to_purchaser fails or times out.

Transform_850_to_PO_Canonical.description: Transform the decoded 850 XML (first good transaction set) to the supplier canonical shape using the project map in Artifacts/Maps (map source LogicApp). goodMessages is a Learn-documented X12 Decode output; the per-item 'body' accessor is community-sourced and flagged for a designer/runtime round-trip.

Parse_Canonical.description: Convert the supplier canonical XML to JSON so scalar fields and the line-item collection can be passed to the SQL upsert proc. json() is a native WDL conversion. Field paths assume the PurchaseOrder canonical contract (root purchaseOrder); the lines collection is at lines/line after XML->JSON. Depends on Simon's map output shape (authored in parallel) - flagged.

Persist_Purchase_Order.description: Normalized persist into the supplier-owned 'sup' schema via [sup].[usp_UpsertPurchaseOrder] (built-in SQL, managed identity, idempotent on PoNumber). The built-in SQL connector cannot pass a table-valued parameter, so line items are passed as a JSON string (LinesJson) for the proc to shred with OPENJSON (verified pattern, decisions.md).
